--- PAGE 3 ---
CONTENTS
With few exceptions, the Sunday Readings are formatted to fit on a SINGLE PAGE:
— C, 9 = Denotes Cycle C readings found on page 9
— ABC, 9 = Denotes readings are identical for Cycles A, B, C
— ABC (2 pages), 9 = Denotes exception to single page for Sunday Readings
For Sundays with READING(S) OPTIONS that exceed one page—where
possible—options are given in sequence to permit one-page-per-Sunday:
— C (2 pages), 9 = Includes all optional readings
(cid:1)
— C, (w/ 1st Reading Option A only), 11 = Denotes text option as described
(cid:1)
— C, (w/ 1st Reading Option B only), 12
EXCEPTIONS to Sunday Readings on a single page: Palm (Passion) Sunday, Good
Friday, Easter Vigil, and Corpus Christi. In addition, the Christmas Vigil (Cycles ABC),
the 3rd, 4th and 5th Sundays of Lent (Cycle A); and the 24th Sunday in Ordinary Time
(Cycle C) are two pages if the longer form of the Gospel is used. All Souls (November 2)
is the only Mass in which there are options for all readings.
PAGE NUMBERS are given in Adobe Acrobat Reader.

1st Sunday of Advent — Year A, page 15
— B, 16
— C, 17
2nd Sunday of Advent — A, 18

CATHOLIC SUNDAY READINGS BROTHERS and sisters:
FIRST SUNDAY OF ADVENT—A You know the time;
it is the hour now for you to awake from sleep.
For our salvation is nearer now than when we first believed;
FIRST READING (Is 2:1-5) the night is advanced, the day is at hand.
Let us then throw off the works of darkness
A reading from the Book of the Prophet Isaiah: and put on the armor of light;
let us conduct ourselves properly as in the day,
THIS is what Isaiah, son of Amoz, not in orgies and drunkenness,
saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem. not in promiscuity and lust,
In days to come, not in rivalry and jealousy.
the mountain of the LORD’s house But put on the Lord Jesus Christ,
shall be established as the highest mountain and make no provision for the desires of the flesh.
and raised above the hills.
All nations shall stream toward it; The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
many peoples shall come and say:
“Come, let us climb the LORD’s mountain, ALLELUIA (cf. Psalm 85:8)
to the house of the God of Jacob,
that he may instruct us in his ways, R. Alleluia, alleluia.
and we may walk in his paths.”
For from Zion shall go forth instruction, Lord, show us your love;
and the word of the LORD from Jerusalem. and grant us your salvation.—R.
He shall judge between the nations,
and impose terms on many peoples. GOSPEL (24:37-44)
They shall beat their swords into plowshares
and their spears into pruning hooks; + A reading from the holy Gospel according to Matthew:
one nation shall not raise the sword against another,
nor shall they train for war again. JESUS said to his disciples:
O house of Jacob, come, “As it was in the days of Noah,
let us walk in the light of the Lord! so it will be at the coming of the Son of Man.
In those days before the flood,
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God. they were eating and drinking,
marrying and giving in marriage,
RESPONSORIAL PSALM (Psalm 122:1-2, 3-4, 4-5, 6-7, 8-9) up to the day that Noah entered the ark.
They did not know until the flood came and carried them all away.
R. Let us go rejoicing to the house of the Lord. So will it be also at the coming of the Son of Man.
Two men will be out in the field;
I rejoiced because they said to me, one will be taken, and one will be left.
“We will go up to the house of the LORD.” Two women will be grinding at the mill;
And now we have set foot one will be taken, and one will be left.
within your gates, O Jerusalem.—R. Therefore, stay awake!
For you do not know on which day your Lord will come.
Jerusalem, built as a city Be sure of this: if the master of the house
with compact unity. had known the hour of night when the thief was coming,
To it the tribes go up, he would have stayed awake
the tribes of the LORD.—R. and not let his house be broken into.
So too, you also must be prepared,
According to the decree for Israel, for at an hour you do not expect, the Son of Man will come.”
to give thanks to the name of the LORD.
In it are set up judgment seats, The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
seats for the house of David.—R.
Pray for the peace of Jerusalem!
May those who love you prosper!
May peace be within your walls,
prosperity in your buildings.—R.
Because of my brothers and friends
I will say, “Peace be within you!”
Because of the house of the LORD, our God,
I will pray for your good.—R.
SECOND READING (Rom 13:11-14)
A reading from the Letter of Saint Paul to the Romans:

CATHOLIC SUNDAY READINGS I give thanks to my God always on your account
FIRST SUNDAY OF ADVENT—B for the grace of God bestowed on you in Christ Jesus,
that in him you were enriched in every way,
with all discourse and all knowledge,
FIRST READING (Is 63:16b-17, 19b; 64:2-7) as the testimony to Christ was confirmed among you,
so that you are not lacking in any spiritual gift
A reading from the Book of the Prophet Isaiah: as you wait for the revelation of our Lord Jesus Christ.
He will keep you firm to the end,
YOU, Lord, are our father, irreproachable on the day of our Lord Jesus Christ.
our redeemer you are named forever. God is faithful,
Why do you let us wander, O Lord, from your ways, and by him you were called to fellowship with his Son,
and harden our hearts so that we fear you not? Jesus Christ our Lord.
Return for the sake of your servants,
the tribes of your heritage. The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
Oh, that you would rend the heavens and come down,
with the mountains quaking before you, ALLELUIA (Psalm 85:8)
while you wrought awesome deeds we could not hope for,
such as they had not heard of from of old. R. Alleluia, alleluia.
No ear has ever heard, no eye ever seen, any God but you
doing such deeds for those who wait for him. Show us Lord, your love;
Would that you might meet us doing right, and grant us your salvation.—R.
that we were mindful of you in our ways!
Behold, you are angry, and we are sinful; GOSPEL (Mk 13:33-37)
all of us have become like unclean people,
all our good deeds are like polluted rags; + A reading from the holy Gospel according to Mark:
we have all withered like leaves,
and our guilt carries us away like the wind. JESUS said to his disciples:
There is none who calls upon your name, “Be watchful! Be alert!
who rouses himself to cling to you; You do not know when the time will come.
for you have hidden your face from us It is like a man traveling abroad.
and have delivered us up to our guilt. He leaves home and places his servants in charge,
Yet, O Lord, you are our father; each with his own work,
we are the clay and you the potter: and orders the gatekeeper to be on the watch.
we are all the work of your hands. Watch, therefore;
you do not know when the Lord of the house is coming,
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God. whether in the evening, or at midnight,
or at cockcrow, or in the morning.
RESPONSORIAL PSALM (Psalm 80:2-3, 15-16, 18-19) May he not come suddenly and find you sleeping.
What I say to you, I say to all: “Watch!”
R. (4) Lord, make us turn to you;
let us see your face and we shall be saved. The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
O shepherd of Israel, hearken,
from your throne upon the cherubim, shine forth.
Rouse your power,
and come to save us.—R.
Once again, O Lord of hosts,
look down from heaven, and see;
take care of this vine,
and protect what your right hand has planted
the son of man whom you yourself made strong.—R.
May your help be with the man of your right hand,
with the son of man whom you yourself made strong.
Then we will no more withdraw from you;
give us new life, and we will call upon your name.—R.
SECOND READING (1 Cor 1:3-9)
A reading from the first Letter of Saint Paul to the Corinthians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
Grace to you and peace from God our Father
and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Text copyright Confraternity of Christian Doctrine, Inc., Washington, D.C. All rights reserved. Reprinted with permission.
See "Catholic Religious Support Handbook" for full copyright notice and use restrictions.

--- PAGE 17 ---
CATHOLIC SUNDAY READINGS Show us, Lord, your love;
FIRST SUNDAY OF ADVENT—C and grant us your salvation.—R.
GOSPEL (Lk 21:25-28, 34-36)
FIRST READING (Jer 33:14-16)
+ A reading from the holy Gospel according to Luke:
A reading from the Book of the Prophet Jeremiah:
JESUS said to his disciples:
THE days are coming, says the Lord, “There will be signs in the sun, the moon, and the stars,
when I will fulfill the promise and on earth nations will be in dismay,
I made to the house of Israel and Judah. perplexed by the roaring of the sea and the waves.
In those days, in that time, People will die of fright
I will raise up for David a just shoot ; in anticipation of what is coming upon the world,
he shall do what is right and just in the land. for the powers of the heavens will be shaken.
In those days Judah shall be safe And then they will see the Son of Man
and Jerusalem shall dwell secure; coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
this is what they shall call her: But when these signs begin to happen,
“The Lord our justice.” stand erect and raise your heads
because your redemption is at hand.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
“Beware that your hearts do not become drowsy
RESPONSORIAL PSALM (Psalm 25:4-5, 8-9, 10, 14) from carousing and drunkenness
and the anxieties of daily life,
R. (1b) To you, O Lord, I lift my soul. and that day catch you by surprise like a trap.
For that day will assault everyone
Your ways, O Lord, make known to me; who lives on the face of the earth.
teach me your paths, Be vigilant at all times
Guide me in your truth and teach me, and pray that you have the strength
for you are God my savior, to escape the tribulations that are imminent
and for you I wait all the day.—R. and to stand before the Son of Man.”
Good and upright is the Lord; The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
thus he shows sinners the way.
He guides the humble to justice,
and teaches the humble his way.—R.
All the paths of the Lord are kindness and constancy
toward those who keep his covenant and his decrees.
The friendship of the Lord is with those who fear him,
and his covenant, for their instruction.—R.
SECOND READING (1 Thes 3:12-4:2)
A reading from the first Letter of Saint Paul to the Thessalonians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
May the Lord make you increase and abound in love
for one another and for all,
just as we have for you,
so as to strengthen your hearts,
to be blameless in holiness before our God and Father
at the coming of our Lord Jesus with all his holy ones. Amen.
Finally, brothers and sisters,
we earnestly ask and exhort you in the Lord Jesus that,
as you received from us
how you should conduct yourselves to please God
—and as you are conducting yourselves—
you do so even more.
For you know what instructions we gave you through the Lord Jesus.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
ALLELUIA (Psalm 85:8)
R. Alleluia, alleluia.
Text copyright Confraternity of Christian Doctrine, Inc., Washington, D.C. All rights reserved. Reprinted with permission.
See "Catholic Religious Support Handbook" for full copyright notice and use restrictions.

CATHOLIC SUNDAY READINGS BROTHERS and sisters:
SECOND SUNDAY OF ADVENT—A Whatever was written previously was written for our instruction,
that by endurance and by the encouragement of the Scriptures
we might have hope.
FIRST READING (Is 11:1-10) May the God of endurance and encouragement
grant you to think in harmony with one another,
A reading from the Book of the Prophet Isaiah: in keeping with Christ Jesus,
that with one accord you may with one voice
ON that day, a shoot shall sprout from the stump of Jesse, glorify the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.
and from his roots a bud shall blossom. Welcome one another, then, as Christ welcomed you,
The spirit of the Lord shall rest upon him: for the glory of God.
a spirit of wisdom and of understanding, For I say that Christ became a minister of the circumcised
a spirit of counsel and of strength, to show God’s truthfulness,
a spirit of knowledge and of fear of the Lord, to confirm the promises to the patriarchs,
and his delight shall be the fear of the Lord. but so that the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy.
Not by appearance shall he judge, As it is written:
nor by hearsay shall he decide, Therefore, I will praise you among the Gentiles
but he shall judge the poor with justice, and sing praises to your name.
and decide aright for the land’s afflicted.
He shall strike the ruthless with the rod of his mouth, The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
and with the breath of his lips he shall slay the wicked.
Justice shall be the band around his waist, ALLELUIA (Luke 3:4, 6)
and faithfulness a belt upon his hips.
Then the wolf shall be a guest of the lamb, R. Alleluia, alleluia.
and the leopard shall lie down with the kid;
the calf and the young lion shall browse together, Prepare the way of the Lord, make straight his paths:
with a little child to guide them. all flesh shall see the salvation of God.—R.
The cow and the bear shall be neighbors,
together their young shall rest; GOSPEL (Mt 3:1-12)
the lion shall eat hay like the ox.
The baby shall play by the cobra’s den, + A reading from the holy Gospel according to Matthew:
and the child lay his hand on the adder’s lair.
There shall be no harm or ruin on all my holy mountain; JOHN the Baptist appeared, preaching in the desert of Judea
for the earth shall be filled with knowledge of the Lord, and saying, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand!”
as water covers the sea. It was of him that the prophet Isaiah had spoken when he said:
On that day, the root of Jesse, A voice of one crying out in the desert,
set up as a signal for the nations, Prepare the way of the Lord,
the Gentiles shall seek out, make straight his paths.
for his dwelling shall be glorious. John wore clothing made of camel’s hair
and had a leather belt around his waist.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God. His food was locusts and wild honey.
At that time Jerusalem, all Judea,
RESPONSORIAL PSALM (Psalm 72:1-2, 7-8, 12-13, 17) and the whole region around the Jordan
were going out to him
R. (cf. 7) Justice shall flourish in his time, and fullness of peace for ever. and were being baptized by him in the Jordan River
as they acknowledged their sins.
O God, with your judgment endow the king,
and with your justice, the king’s son; When he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees
he shall govern your people with justice coming to his baptism, he said to them, “You brood of vipers!
and your afflicted ones with judgment.—R. Who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?
Produce good fruit as evidence of your repentance.
Justice shall flower in his days, And do not presume to say to yourselves,
and profound peace, till the moon be no more. ‘We have Abraham as our father.’
May he rule from sea to sea, For I tell you,
and from the River to the ends of the earth.—R. God can raise up children to Abraham from these stones.
Even now the ax lies at the root of the trees.
For he shall rescue the poor when he cries out, Therefore every tree that does not bear good fruit
and the afflicted when he has no one to help him. will be cut down and thrown into the fire.
He shall have pity for the lowly and the poor; I am baptizing you with water, for repentance,
the lives of the poor he shall save.—R. but the one who is coming after me is mightier than I.
I am not worthy to carry his sandals.
May his name be blessed forever; He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and fire.
as long as the sun his name shall remain. His winnowing fan is in his hand.
In him shall all the tribes of the earth be blessed; He will clear his threshing floor
all the nations shall proclaim his happiness.—R. and gather his wheat into his barn,
but the chaff he will burn with unquenchable fire.”
SECOND READING (Rom 15:4-9)
The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
A reading from the Letter of Saint Paul to the Romans:

CATHOLIC SUNDAY READINGS SECOND READING (2 Pt 3:8-14)
SECOND SUNDAY OF ADVENT—B
A reading from the second Letter of Saint Peter
FIRST READING (Is 40:1-5, 9-11) DO not ignore this one fact, beloved,
that with the Lord one day is like a thousand years
A reading from the Book of the Prophet Isaiah: and a thousand years like one day.
The Lord does not delay his promise, as some regard “delay,”
COMFORT, give comfort to my people, but he is patient with you,
says your God. not wishing that any should perish
Speak tenderly to Jerusalem, and proclaim to her but that all should come to repentance.
that her service is at an end, But the day of the Lord will come like a thief,
her guilt is expiated; and then the heavens will pass away with a mighty roar
indeed, she has received from the hand of the LORD and the elements will be dissolved by fire,
double for all her sins. and the earth and everything done on it will be found out.
A voice cries out: Since everything is to be dissolved in this way,
In the desert prepare the way of the LORD! what sort of persons ought you to be,
Make straight in the wasteland a highway for our God! conducting yourselves in holiness and devotion,
Every valley shall be filled in, waiting for and hastening the coming of the day of God,
every mountain and hill shall be made low; because of which the heavens will be dissolved in flames
the rugged land shall be made a plain, and the elements melted by fire.
the rough country, a broad valley. But according to his promise
Then the glory of the LORD shall be revealed, we await new heavens and a new earth
and all people shall see it together; in which righteousness dwells.
for the mouth of the LORD has spoken. Therefore, beloved, since you await these things,
be eager to be found without spot or blemish before him, at peace.
Go up on to a high mountain,
Zion, herald of glad tidings; The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
cry out at the top of your voice,
Jerusalem, herald of good news! ALLELUIA (Luke 3:4, 6)
Fear not to cry out
and say to the cities of Judah: R. Alleluia, alleluia.
Here is your God!
Here comes with power Prepare the way of the Lord, make straight his paths:

SECOND READING (1 Thes 5:16-24)
The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
A reading from the first Letter of Saint Paul to the Thessalonians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
Rejoice always. Pray without ceasing.
In all circumstances give thanks,
for this is the will of God for you in Christ Jesus.
Do not quench the Spirit.
Do not despise prophetic utterances.
Test everything; retain what is good.
Refrain from every kind of evil.
May the God of peace make you perfectly holy
and may you entirely, spirit, soul, and body,
be preserved blameless for the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ.
The one who calls you is faithful,
and he will also accomplish it.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

SECOND READING (Rom 1:1-7)
The beginning of the Letter of Saint Paul to the Romans:
PAUL, a slave of Christ Jesus,
called to be an apostle and set apart for the gospel of God,
which he promised previously through his prophets in the
holy Scriptures,
the gospel about his Son, descended from David according to the flesh,
but established as Son of God in power
according to the Spirit of holiness
through resurrection from the dead, Jesus Christ our Lord.
Through him we have received the grace of apostleship,
to bring about the obedience of faith,
for the sake of his name, among all the Gentiles,
among whom are you also, who are called to belong to Jesus Christ;
to all the beloved of God in Rome, called to be holy.
Grace to you and peace from God our Father
and the Lord Jesus Christ.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

SECOND READING (Heb 10:5-10)
A reading from the Letter to the Hebrews:
BROTHERS and sisters:
When Christ came into the world, he said:
“Sacrifice and offering you did not desire,
but a body you prepared for me;
in holocausts and sin offerings you took no delight.
Then I said, ‘As is written of me in the scroll,
behold, I come to do your will, O God.’”
First he says, “Sacrifices and offerings,
holocausts and sin offerings,
you neither desired nor delighted in.”
These are offered according to the law.
Then he says, “Behold, I come to do your will.”
He takes away the first to establish the second.

Jechoniah became the father of Shealtiel,
Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel,
Zerubbabel the father of Abiud.
Abiud became the father of Eliakim,
Eliakim the father of Azor,
Azor the father of Zadok.
Zadok became the father of Achim,
Achim the father of Eliud,
Eliud the father of Eleazar.
Eleazar became the father of Matthan,
Matthan the father of Jacob,
Jacob the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary.
Of her was born Jesus who is called the Christ.
Thus the total number of generations
from Abraham to David
is fourteen generations;
from David to the Babylonian exile,
fourteen generations;
from the Babylonian exile to the Christ,
fourteen generations.
[ Now this is how the birth of Jesus Christ came about.
When his mother Mary was betrothed to Joseph,
but before they lived together,
she was found with child through the Holy Spirit.
Joseph her husband, since he was a righteous man,
yet unwilling to expose her to shame,
decided to divorce her quietly.
Such was his intention when, behold,
the angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said,
“Joseph, son of David,
do not be afraid to take Mary your wife into your home.
For it is through the Holy Spirit
that this child has been conceived in her.
She will bear a son and you are to name him Jesus,
because he will save his people from their sins.”
All this took place to fulfill
what the Lord had said through the prophet:
Behold, the virgin shall conceive and bear a son,
and they shall name him Emmanuel,
which means “God is with us.”
When Joseph awoke,
he did as the angel of the Lord had commanded him
and took his wife into his home.
He had no relations with her until she bore a son,
and he named him Jesus. ]
The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.

RESPONSORIAL PSALM (Psalm 97:1, 6, 11-12)
R. A light will shine on us this day: the Lord is born for us.
The LORD is king; let the earth rejoice;
let the many islands be glad.
The heavens proclaim his justice,
and all peoples see his glory.—R.
Light dawns for the just;
and gladness, for the upright of heart.
Be glad in the LORD, you just,
and give thanks to his holy name.—R.
SECOND READING (Ti 3:4-7)
A reading from the Letter of Saint Paul to Titus:
BELOVED:
When the kindness and generous love
of God our savior appeared,
not because of any righteous deeds we had done
but because of his mercy,
He saved us through the bath of rebirth
and renewal by the Holy Spirit,
whom he richly poured out on us
through Jesus Christ our savior,
so that we might be justified by his grace
and become heirs in hope of eternal life.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

CATHOLIC SUNDAY READINGS Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly,
HOLY FAMILY—ABC (Gospel A) as in all wisdom you teach and admonish one another,
singing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs
with gratitude in your hearts to God.
FIRST READING (Sir 3:2-7, 12-14) And whatever you do, in word or in deed,
do everything in the name of the Lord Jesus,
A reading from the Book of Sirach: giving thanks to God the Father through him. ]
GOD sets a father in honor over his children; Wives, be subordinate to your husbands,
a mother’s authority he confirms over her sons. as is proper in the Lord.
Whoever honors his father atones for sins, Husbands, love your wives,
and preserves himself from them. and avoid any bitterness toward them.
When he prays, he is heard; Children, obey your parents in everything,

You who have no money,
come, receive grain and eat; A reading from the first Letter of Saint John:

you shall delight in rich fare. In this way we know that we love the children of God

listen, that you may have life. For the love of God is this,

the benefits assured to David. And his commandments are not burdensome,

because of the LORD, your God, This is the one who came through water and blood, Jesus Christ,

call him while he is near. So there are three that testify,

and the wicked man his thoughts; and the three are of one accord.
let him turn to the LORD for mercy; If we accept human testimony,
to our God, who is generous in forgiving. the testimony of God is surely greater.
For my thoughts are not your thoughts, Now the testimony of God is this,
nor are your ways my ways, says the LORD. that he has testified on behalf of his Son.

so high are my ways above your ways The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

and the one you have now is not your husband.
What you have said is true.”
The woman said to him,
[ “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.
Our ancestors worshiped on this mountain;
but you people say that the place to worship is in Jerusalem.”
Jesus said to her,
“Believe me, woman, the hour is coming
when you will worship the Father
neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem.
You people worship what you do not understand;
we worship what we understand,
because salvation is from the Jews.
But the hour is coming, and is now here,
when true worshipers will worship the Father in Spirit and truth;
and indeed the Father seeks such people to worship him.
God is Spirit, and those who worship him
must worship in Spirit and truth.”
The woman said to him,
“I know that the Messiah is coming, the one called the Christ;
when he comes, he will tell us everything.”
Jesus said to her,
“I am he, the one speaking with you.” ]
At that moment his disciples returned,
and were amazed that he was talking with a woman,
but still no one said, “What are you looking for?”
or “Why are you talking with her?”
The woman left her water jar
and went into the town and said to the people,
“Come see a man who told me everything I have done.
Could he possibly be the Christ?”
They went out of the town and came to him.
Meanwhile, the disciples urged him, “Rabbi, eat.”
But he said to them,
“I have food to eat of which you do not know.”
So the disciples said to one another,
“Could someone have brought him something to eat?”
Jesus said to them,
“My food is to do the will of the one who sent me
and to finish his work.
Do you not say, ‘In four months the harvest will be here’?
I tell you, look up and see the fields ripe for the harvest.
The reaper is already receiving payment
and gathering crops for eternal life,
so that the sower and reaper can rejoice together.
For here the saying is verified that ‘One sows and another reaps.’
I sent you to reap what you have not worked for;
others have done the work,
and you are sharing the fruits of their work.”
[ Many of the Samaritans of that town began to believe in him
because of the word of the woman who testified,
“He told me everything I have done.”
When the Samaritans came to him,
they invited him to stay with them;
and he stayed there two days.
Many more began to believe in him because of his word,
and they said to the woman,
“We no longer believe because of your word;
for we have heard for ourselves,
and we know that this is truly the savior of the world.” ]
The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.

though perhaps for a good person one might even find courage to die. and they said to the woman,
But God proves his love for us “We no longer believe because of your word;

and we know that this is truly the savior of the world.”

we are disciples of Moses!

that God has prepared in advance,

THE LORD said to Joshua, “A man had two sons, and the younger son said to his father,
“Today I have removed the reproach of Egypt from you.” ‘Father give me the share of your estate that should come to me.’
So the father divided the property between them.
While the Israelites were encamped at Gilgal on the plains of Jericho, After a few days, the younger son collected all his belongings
they celebrated the Passover and set off to a distant country
on the evening of the fourteenth of the month. where he squandered his inheritance on a life of dissipation.
On the day after the Passover, When he had freely spent everything,
they ate of the produce of the land a severe famine struck that country,
in the form of unleavened cakes and parched grain. and he found himself in dire need.

on which they ate of the produce of the land, the manna ceased. who sent him to his farm to tend the swine.

From noon onward, darkness came over the whole land
until three in the afternoon.
And about three oclock Jesus cried out in a loud voice,
"Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?"
which means, "My God, my God, why have you forsaken me?"
Some of the bystanders who heard it said,
"This one is calling for Elijah."
Immediately one of them ran to get a sponge;
he soaked it in wine, and putting it on a reed,
gave it to him to drink.
But the rest said,
"Wait, let us see if Elijah comes to save him"
But Jesus cried out again in a loud voice,
and gave up his spirit.
[ Here all kneel and pause for a short time. ]
And behold, the veil of the sanctuary
was torn in two from top to bottom.
The earth quaked, rocks were split, tombs were opened,
and the bodies of many saints who had fallen asleep
were raised.
And coming forth from their tombs after his resurrection,
they entered the holy city and appeared to many.
The centurion and the men with him who were keeping watch
over Jesus
feared greatly when they saw the earthquake
and all that was happening, and they said,
"Truly, this was the Son of God!" ]
There were many women there, looking on from a distance,
who had followed Jesus from Galilee, ministering to him.
Among them were Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of James
and Joseph,
and the mother of the sons of Zebedee.
When it was evening,
there came a rich man from Arimathea named Joseph,
who was himself a disciple of Jesus.
He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus;
then Pilate ordered it to be handed over.

"Wait, let us see if Elijah comes to take him down."

There were also many other women
who had come up with him to Jerusalem. ]
When it was already evening,
since it was the day of preparation,
the day before the sabbath, Joseph of Arimathea,
a distinguished member of the council,
who was himself awaiting the kingdom of God,
came and courageously went to Pilate
and asked for the body of Jesus.
Pilate was amazed that he was already dead.
He summoned the centurion
and asked him if Jesus had already died.
And when he learned of it from the centurion,
he gave the body to Joseph.
Having bought a linen cloth, he took him down,
wrapped him in the linen cloth,
and laid him in a tomb that had been hewn out of the rock.
Then he rolled a stone against the entrance to the tomb.
Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joses
watched where he was laid.
The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.

He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
After he had taken the body down,
he wrapped it in a linen cloth
and laid him in a rock-hewn tomb
in which no one had yet been buried.
It was the day of preparation,
and the sabbath was about to begin.
The women who had come from Galilee with him followed behind,
and when they had seen the tomb
and the way in which his body was laid in it,
they returned and prepared spices and perfumed oils.
Then they rested on the sabbath according to the commandment.
The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.

Now in the place where he had been crucified there was a garden,
and in the garden a new tomb, in which no one had yet been buried.
So they laid Jesus there because of the Jewish preparation day;
for the tomb was close by.

God made the two great lights,
Nine readings are assigned to the Easter Vigil: seven from the OT and two the greater one to govern the day,
from the NT. If circumstances demand in individual cases, the number of and the lesser one to govern the night;
prescribed readings may be reduced. Three selections from the OT, and he made the stars.
however, should be read before the epistle and Gospel, although when God set them in the dome of the sky,
necessary, two may be read. In any case, the reading from Exodus about to shed light upon the earth,
the escape through the Red Sea (reading 3) should never be omitted. to govern the day and the night,
and to separate the light from the darkness.

listen, that you may have life. How is it, Israel,

to our God, who is generous in forgiving.
For my thoughts are not your thoughts, The One who knows all things knows her;
nor are your ways my ways, says the LORD. he has probed her by his knowledge—
As high as the heavens are above the earth, The One who established the earth for all time,
so high are my ways above your ways and filled it with four-footed beasts;
and my thoughts above your thoughts. he who dismisses the light, and it departs,
calls it, and it obeys him trembling;
For just as from the heavens before whom the stars at their posts
the rain and snow come down shine and rejoice;
and do not return there when he calls them, they answer, “Here we are!”
till they have watered the earth, shining with joy for their Maker.
making it fertile and fruitful, Such is our God;
giving seed to the one who sows no other is to be compared to him:
and bread to the one who eats, He has traced out the whole way of understanding,
so shall my word be and has given her to Jacob, his servant,

BELOVED: But these are written that you may come to believe
Everyone who believes that Jesus is the Christ is begotten by God, that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God,
and everyone who loves the Father and that through this belief you may have life in his name.
loves also the one begotten by him.
In this way we know that we love the children of God The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
when we love God and obey his commandments.
For the love of God is this,
that we keep his commandments.
And his commandments are not burdensome,
for whoever is begotten by God conquers the world.
And the victory that conquers the world is our faith.
Who indeed is the victor over the world
but the one who believes that Jesus is the Son of God?

MY children, I am writing this to you
so that you may not commit sin.
But if anyone does sin, we have an Advocate with the Father,
Jesus Christ the righteous one.
He is expiation for our sins,
and not for our sins only but for those of the whole world.
The way we may be sure that we know him is to keep
his commandments.
Those who say, "I know him, " but do not keep his commandments
are liars, and the truth is not in them.
But whoever keeps his word,
the love of God is truly perfected in him.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

not that we have loved God, but that he loved us
and sent his Son as expiation for our sins.

A reading from the first Letter of Saint Peter:
BELOVED:
Rejoice to the extent that you share in the sufferings of Christ,
so that when his glory is revealed
you may also rejoice exultantly.
If you are insulted for the name of Christ, blessed are you,
for the Spirit of glory and of God rests upon you.
But let no one among you be made to suffer
as a murderer, a thief, an evildoer, or as an intriguer.
But whoever is made to suffer as a Christian should not be ashamed
but glorify God because of the name.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

CATHOLIC SUNDAY READINGS BROTHERS and sisters:
PENTECOST SUNDAY (MASS DURING THE DAY)—C No one can say, “Jesus is Lord, ” except by the Holy Spirit.
There are different kinds of spiritual gifts but the same Spirit;
there are different forms of service but the same Lord;
FIRST READING there are different workings but the same God
who produces all of them in everyone.
A reading from the Acts of the Apostles (Acts 2:1-11) To each individual the manifestation of the Spirit
is given for some benefit.
WHEN the time for Pentecost was fulfilled,
they were all in one place together. As a body is one though it has many parts,
And suddenly there came from the sky and all the parts of the body, though many, are one body,
a noise like a strong driving wind, so also Christ.
and it filled the entire house in which they were. For in one Spirit we were all baptized into one body,
Then there appeared to them tongues as of fire, whether Jews or Greeks, slaves or free persons,
which parted and came to rest on each one of them. and we were all given to drink of one Spirit.
And they were all filled with the Holy Spirit
and began to speak in different tongues, The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
as the Spirit enabled them to proclaim.

Now there were devout Jews from every nation under heaven
staying in Jerusalem. B (Rom 8:8-17)
At this sound, they gathered in a large crowd,
but they were confused A reading from the Letter of Saint Paul to the Romans:
because each one heard them speaking in his own language.
They were astounded, and in amazement they asked, BROTHERS and sisters:
“Are not all these people who are speaking Galileans? Those who are in the flesh cannot please God.
Then how does each of us hear them in his native language? But you are not in the flesh;
We are Parthians, Medes, and Elamites, on the contrary, you are in the spirit,
inhabitants of Mesopotamia, Judea and Cappadocia, if only the Spirit of God dwells in you.
Pontus and Asia, Phrygia and Pamphylia, Whoever does not have the Spirit of Christ does not belong to him.
Egypt and the districts of Libya near Cyrene, But if Christ is in you,
as well as travelers from Rome, although the body is dead because of sin,
both Jews and converts to Judaism, Cretans and Arabs, the spirit is alive because of righteousness.
yet we hear them speaking in our own tongues If the Spirit of the one who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you,
of the mighty acts of God.” the one who raised Christ from the dead
will give life to your mortal bodies also,
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God. through his Spirit that dwells in you.
Consequently, brothers and sisters,

to live according to the flesh.

or: but if by the Spirit you put to death the deeds of the body,

but that you be united in the same mind and in the same purpose.

by Chloe's people, that there are rivalries among you.
I mean that each of you is saying,
"I belong to Paul," or "I belong to Apollos,"
or "I belong to Cephas," or "I belong to Christ."
Is Christ divided?

CATHOLIC SUNDAY READINGS If a foot should say,
"Because I am not a hand I do not belong to the body,"
THIRD SUNDAY IN ORDINARY TIME—C
it does not for this reason belong any less to the body.
Or if an ear should say,
"Because I am not an eye I do not belong to the body,"
FIRST READING (Neh 8:2-4a, 5-6, 8-10) it does not for this reason belong any less to the body.
If the whole body were an eye, where would the hearing be?
A reading from the Book of Nehemiah: If the whole body were hearing, where would the sense of smell be?
But as it is, God placed the parts,
EZRA the priest brought the law before the assembly, each one of them, in the body as he intended.
which consisted of men, women, If they were all one part, where would the body be?
and those children old enough to understand. But as it is, there are many parts, yet one body.
Standing at one end of the open place that was before the Water Gate, The eye cannot say to the hand, "I do not need you,"
he read out of the book from daybreak till midday, nor again the head to the feet, "I do not need you."
in the presence of the men, the women, Indeed, the parts of the body that seem to be weaker
and those children old enough to understand; are all the more necessary,
and all the people listened attentively to the book of the law. and those parts of the body that we consider less honorable
Ezra the scribe stood on a wooden platform we surround with greater honor,
that had been made for the occasion. and our less presentable parts are treated with greater propriety,
He opened the scroll whereas our more presentable parts do not need this.
so that all the people might see it But God has so constructed the body
—for he was standing higher up than any of the people—; as to give greater honor to a part that is without it,
and, as he opened it, all the people rose. so that there may be no division in the body,
Ezra blessed the LORD, the great God, but that the parts may have the same concern for one another.
and all the people, their hands raised high, answered, If one part suffers, all the parts suffer with it;
"Amen, amen!" if one part is honored, all the parts share its joy.
Then they bowed down and prostrated themselves before the LORD,
their faces to the ground. Now [ you are Christ's body, and individually parts of it. ]
Ezra read plainly from the book of the law of God, Some people God has designated in the church
interpreting it so that all could understand what was read. to be, first, apostles; second, prophets; third, teachers;
Then Nehemiah, that is, His Excellency, and Ezra the priest-scribe then, mighty deeds;
and the Levites who were instructing the people then gifts of healing, assistance, administration,
said to all the people: and varieties of tongues.
"Today is holy to the LORD your God. Are all apostles? Are all prophets? Are all teachers?
Do not be sad, and do not weep"— Do all work mighty deeds? Do all have gifts of healing?
for all the people were weeping as they heard the words of the law. Do all speak in tongues? Do all interpret?
He said further: "Go, eat rich foods and drink sweet drinks,
and allot portions to those who had nothing prepared; The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
for today is holy to our LORD.
Do not be saddened this day, ALLELUIA (cf. Luke 4:18)
for rejoicing in the LORD must be your strength!"

so that no human being might boast before God.

who became for us wisdom from God,

SECOND READING (1 Cor 7:32-35)
A reading from the first Letter of Saint Paul to the Corinthians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
I should like you to be free of anxieties.
An unmarried man is anxious about the things of the Lord,
how he may please the Lord.
But a married man is anxious about the things of the world,
how he may please his wife, and he is divided.
An unmarried woman or a virgin is anxious about the things of the Lord,
so that she may be holy in both body and spirit.
A married woman, on the other hand,
is anxious about the things of the world,
how she may please her husband.
I am telling you this for your own benefit,
not to impose a restraint upon you,
but for the sake of propriety

who conducts his affairs with justice.—R.
He shall never be moved;
the just one shall be in everlasting remembrance.
An evil report he shall not fear;
his heart is firm, trusting in the LORD.—R.
His heart is steadfast; he shall not fear.
Lavishly he gives to the poor;
His justice shall endure forever;
his horn shall be exalted in glory.—R.
SECOND READING (1 Cor 2:1-5)
A reading from the first Letter of Saint Paul to the Corinthians:
WHEN I came to you, brothers and sisters,
proclaiming the mystery of God,
I did not come with sublimity of words or of wisdom.
For I resolved to know nothing while I was with you
except Jesus Christ, and him crucified.
I came to you in weakness and fear and much trembling,
and my message and my proclamation
were not with persuasive words of wisdom,
but with a demonstration of Spirit and power,
so that your faith might rest not on human wisdom
but on the power of God.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

SECOND READING (1 Cor 9:16-19, 22-23)
A reading from the first Letter of Saint Paul to the Corinthians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
If I preach the gospel, this is no reason for me to boast,
for an obligation has been imposed on me,
and woe to me if I do not preach it!
If I do so willingly, I have a recompense,
but if unwillingly, then I have been entrusted with a stewardship.
What then is my recompense?
That, when I preach,
I offer the gospel free of charge
so as not to make full use of my right in the gospel.
Although I am free in regard to all,
I have made myself a slave to all
so as to win over as many as possible.
To the weak I became weak, to win over the weak.
I have become all things to all, to save at least some.

If for this life only we have hoped in Christ,
we are the most pitiable people of all.
But now Christ has been raised from the dead,
the firstfruits of those who have fallen asleep.

that they are vain.

SECOND READING (1 Cor 1:18-22)
A reading from the second Letter of Saint Paul to the Corinthians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
As God is faithful,
our word to you is not “yes” and “no.”
For the Son of God, Jesus Christ,
who was proclaimed to you by us, Silvanus and Timothy and me,
was not “yes” and “no,” but “yes” has been in him.
For however many are the promises of God, their Yes is in him;
therefore, the Amen from us also goes through him to God for glory.
But the one who gives us security with you in Christ
and who anointed us is God;
he has also put his seal upon us
and given the Spirit in our hearts as a first installment.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

CATHOLIC SUNDAY READINGS GOSPEL (Mt 6:24-34)
EIGHTH SUNDAY IN ORDINARY TIME—A
+ A reading from the holy Gospel according to Matthew:
FIRST READING (Is 49:14-15) JESUS said to his disciples:
“No one can serve two masters.
A reading from the Book of the Prophet Isaiah: He will either hate one and love the other,
or be devoted to one and despise the other.
ZION said, “The Lord has forsaken me; You cannot serve God and mammon.
my Lord has forgotten me.”
Can a mother forget her infant, “Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life,
be without tenderness for the child of her womb? what you will eat or drink,
Even should she forget, or about your body, what you will wear.
I will never forget you. Is not life more than food and the body more than clothing?
Look at the birds in the sky;
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God. they do not sow or reap, they gather nothing into barns,
yet your heavenly Father feeds them.
RESPONSORIAL PSALM (Psalm 62:2-3, 6-7, 8-9) Are not you more important than they?
Can any of you by worrying add a single moment to your life-span?
R. (6a) Rest in God alone, my soul. Why are you anxious about clothes?
Learn from the way the wild flowers grow.
Only in God is my soul at rest; They do not work or spin.
from him comes my salvation. But I tell you that not even Solomon in all his splendor
He only is my rock and my salvation, was clothed like one of them.
my stronghold; I shall not be disturbed at all.—R. If God so clothes the grass of the field,
which grows today and is thrown into the oven tomorrow,
Only in God be at rest, my soul, will he not much more provide for you, O you of little faith?
for from him comes my hope. So do not worry and say, ‘What are we to eat?’
He only is my rock and my salvation, or ‘What are we to drink?’or ‘What are we to wear?’
my stronghold; I shall not be disturbed.—R. All these things the pagans seek.
Your heavenly Father knows that you need them all.
With God is my safety and my glory, But seek first the kingdom of God and his righteousness,
he is the rock of my strength; my refuge is in God. and all these things will be given you besides.
Trust in him at all times, O my people! Do not worry about tomorrow; tomorrow will take care of itself.
Pour out your hearts before him.—R. Sufficient for a day is its own evil.”
SECOND READING (1 Cor 4:1-5) The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
A reading from the first Letter of Saint Paul to the Corinthians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
Thus should one regard us: as servants of Christ
and stewards of the mysteries of God.
Now it is of course required of stewards
that they be found trustworthy.
It does not concern me in the least
that I be judged by you or any human tribunal;
I do not even pass judgment on myself;
I am not conscious of anything against me,
but I do not thereby stand acquitted;
the one who judges me is the Lord.
Therefore do not make any judgment before the appointed time,
until the Lord comes,
for he will bring to light what is hidden in darkness
and will manifest the motives of our hearts,
and then everyone will receive praise from God.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
ALLELUIA (Heb 4:12)
R. Alleluia, alleluia.
The word of God is living and effective;
discerning reflections and thoughts of the heart.—R.

SECOND READING (2 Cor 3:1b-6)
A reading from the second Letter of Saint Paul to the Corinthians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
Do we need, as some do,
letters of recommendation to you or from you?
You are our letter, written on our hearts,
known and read by all,
shown to be a letter of Christ ministered by us,
written not in ink but by the Spirit of the living God,
not on tablets of stone but on tablets that are hearts of flesh.
Such confidence we have through Christ toward God.
Not that of ourselves we are qualified
to take credit for anything as coming from us;
rather, our qualification comes from God,
who has indeed qualified us as ministers of a new covenant,
not of letter but of spirit;
for the letter brings death, but the Spirit gives life.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

As the test of what the potter molds is in the furnace, Why do you notice the splinter in your brother’s eye,
so in tribulation is the test of the just. but do not perceive the wooden beam in your own?

so too does one’s speech disclose the bent of one’s mind. ‘Brother, let me remove that splinter in your eye,’

for it is then that people are tested. You hypocrite! Remove the wooden beam from your eye first;
then you will see clearly

and this which is mortal clothes itself with immortality,

Death is swallowed up in victory.
Where, O death, is your victory?

The sting of death is sin,
and the power of sin is the law.
But thanks be to God who gives us the victory
through our Lord Jesus Christ.
Therefore, my beloved brothers and sisters,
be firm, steadfast, always fully devoted to the work of the Lord,
knowing that in the Lord your labor is not in vain.

The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
PAUL, an apostle not from human beings nor through a human being
but through Jesus Christ and God the Father
who raised him from the dead,
and all the brothers who are with me,
to the churches of Galatia.
I am amazed that you are so quickly forsaking
the one who called you by the grace of Christ
for a different gospel - not that there is another.
But there are some who are disturbing you
and wish to pervert the gospel of Christ.
But even if we or an angel from heaven
should preach to you a gospel
other than the one that we preached to you,
let that one be accursed!
As we have said before, and now I say again,
if anyone preaches to you a gospel
other than what you have received, let that one be accursed!
Am I now currying favor with humans or with God?
Or am I seeking to please people?
If I were still trying to please people,
I would not be a slave of Christ.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

for it is love that I desire, not sacrifice, He heard this and said,
and knowledge of God rather than holocausts. “Those who are well do not need a physician, but the sick do.

The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God. ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice.’
I did not come to call the righteous but sinners.”
RESPONSORIAL PSALM Psalm 50:1, 8, 12-13, 14-15
The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
R. (23b) To the upright I will show the saving power of God.
God the Lord has spoken and summoned the earth,
from the rising of the sun to its setting.
“Not for your sacrifices do I rebuke you,
for your holocausts are before me always.”—R.
“If I were hungry, I would not tell you,
for mine are the world and its fullness.
Do I eat the flesh of strong bulls,
or is the blood of goats my drink?”—R.
“Offer to God praise as your sacrifice
and fulfill your vows to the Most High;
Then call upon me in time of distress;
I will rescue you, and you shall glorify me.”—R.
SECOND READING (Rom 4:18-25)
A reading from the Letter of Saint Paul to the Romans:
BROTHERS and sisters:
Abraham believed, hoping against hope,
that he would become “the father of many nations,”
according to what was said, “Thus shall your descendants be.”
He did not weaken in faith when he considered his own body
as already dead—for he was almost a hundred years old—
and the dead womb of Sarah.
He did not doubt God’s promise in unbelief;
rather, he was strengthened by faith and gave glory to God
and was fully convinced that what he had promised
he was also able to do.
That is why it was credited to him as righteousness.
But it was not for him alone that it was written
that it was credited to him;
it was also for us, to whom it will be credited,
who believe in the one who raised Jesus our Lord from the dead,
who was handed over for our transgressions
and was raised for our justification.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

SECOND READING (Rom 5:6-11)
A reading from the Letter of Saint Paul to the Romans:
BROTHERS and sisters:
Christ, while we were still helpless,
yet died at the appointed time for the ungodly.
Indeed, only with difficulty does one die for a just person,
though perhaps for a good person
one might even find courage to die.
But God proves his love for us
in that while we were still sinners Christ died for us.
How much more then, since we are now justified by his blood,
will we be saved through him from the wrath.
Indeed, if, while we were enemies,
we were reconciled to God through the death of his Son,
how much more, once reconciled,
will we be saved by his life.
Not only that,
but we also boast of God through our Lord Jesus Christ,
through whom we have now received reconciliation.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

SECOND READING (2 Cor 5:6-10)
A reading from the second Letter of Saint Paul to the Corinthians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
We are always courageous,
although we know that while we are at home in the body
we are away from the Lord,
for we walk by faith, not by sight.
Yet we are courageous,
and we would rather leave the body and go home to the Lord.
Therefore, we aspire to please him,
whether we are at home or away.
For we must all appear before the judgment seat of Christ,
so that each may receive recompense,
according to what he did in the body, whether good or evil.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

SECOND READING (2 Cor 5:14-17)
A reading from the second Letter of Saint Paul to the Corinthians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
The love of Christ impels us,
once we have come to the conviction that one died for all;
therefore, all have died.
He indeed died for all,
so that those who live might no longer live for themselves
but for him who for their sake died and was raised.
Consequently, from now on we regard no one according to the flesh;
even if we once knew Christ according to the flesh,
yet now we know him so no longer.
So whoever is in Christ is a new creation:
the old things have passed away;
behold, new things have come.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

SECOND READING (Rom 6:3-4, 8-11)
A reading from the Letter of Saint Paul to the Romans:
BROTHERS and sisters:
Are you unaware that we who were baptized into Christ Jesus
were baptized into his death?
We were indeed buried with him through baptism into death,
so that, just as Christ was raised from the dead
by the glory of the Father,
we too might live in newness of life.
If, then, we have died with Christ,
we believe that we shall also live with him.
We know that Christ, raised from the dead, dies no more;
death no longer has power over him.
As to his death, he died to sin once and for all;
as to his life, he lives for God.
Consequently, you too must think of yourselves as dead to sin

the one who raised Christ from the dead
will give life to your mortal bodies also,
through his Spirit that dwells in you.
Consequently, brothers and sisters,
we are not debtors to the flesh,
to live according to the flesh.

SECOND READING (2 Cor 12:7-10)

BROTHERS and sisters:
That I, Paul, might not become too elated,
because of the abundance of the revelations,
a thorn in the flesh was given to me, an angel of Satan,
to beat me, to keep me from being too elated.
Three times I begged the Lord about this, that it might leave me,
but he said to me, "My grace is sufficient for you,
for power is made perfect in weakness."
I will rather boast most gladly of my weaknesses,
in order that the power of Christ may dwell with me.
Therefore, I am content with weaknesses, insults,
hardships, persecutions, and constraints,
for the sake of Christ;
for when I am weak, then I am strong.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

Just as from the heavens

and do not return there
and the whole crowd stood along the shore.
till they have watered the earth,

giving seed to the one who sows
And as he sowed, some seed fell on the path,
and bread to the one who eats,
and birds came and ate it up.
so shall my word be
Some fell on rocky ground, where it had little soil.
that goes forth from my mouth;
It sprang up at once because the soil was not deep,
my word shall not return to me void,
and when the sun rose it was scorched,
but shall do my will,
and it withered for lack of roots.
achieving the end for which I sent it.
Some seed fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked it.

The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks to be God.
a hundred or sixty or thirtyfold.
Whoever has ears ought to hear." ]

from anyone who has not, even what he has will be taken away.

Thus have you prepared the land: drenching its furrows,

breaking up its clods,

Softening it with showers,

blessing its yield.—R.
you shall indeed look but never see.
Gross is the heart of this people,

and hear with their ears

and the valleys blanketed with grain.

BROTHERS and sisters:

I consider that the sufferings of this present time are as nothing

compared with the glory to be revealed for us.
who hears the word of the kingdom without understanding it,
For creation awaits with eager expectation
and the evil one comes and steals away
the revelation of the children of God;
what was sown in his heart.
for creation was made subject to futility,
The seed sown on rocky ground
not of its own accord but because of the one who subjected it,
is the one who hears the word and receives it at once with joy.
in hope that creation itself
But he has no root and lasts only for a time.
would be set free from slavery to corruption
When some tribulation or persecution comes because of the word,
and share in the glorious freedom of the children of God.
he immediately falls away.
We know that all creation is groaning in labor pains even until now;
The seed sown among thorns is the one who hears the word,
and not only that, but we ourselves,
but then worldly anxiety and the lure of riches choke the word
who have the firstfruits of the Spirit,
and it bears no fruit.
we also groan within ourselves
But the seed sown on rich soil
as we wait for adoption, the redemption of our bodies.
is the one who hears the word and understands it,
who indeed bears fruit and yields a hundred or sixty or thirtyfold."

BROTHERS and sisters:
What will separate us from the love of Christ?

through him who loved us.

nor angels, nor principalities,
nor present things, nor future things,
nor powers, nor height, nor depth,
nor any other creature will be able to separate us
from the love of God in Christ Jesus our Lord.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

theirs the patriarchs, and from them,
according to the flesh, is the Christ,
who is over all, God blessed forever. Amen.

SECOND READING (Eph 4:30-5:2)
A reading from the Letter of Saint Paul to the Ephesians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
Do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God,
with which you were sealed for the day of redemption.
All bitterness, fury, anger, shouting, and reviling
must be removed from you, along with all malice.
And be kind to one another, compassionate,
forgiving one another as God has forgiven you in Christ.
So be imitators of God, as beloved children, and live in love,
as Christ loved us and handed himself over for us
as a sacrificial offering to God for a fragrant aroma.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

SECOND READING (Rom 11:13-15, 29-32)
A reading from the Letter of Saint Paul to the Romans:
BROTHERS and sisters:
I am speaking to you Gentiles.
Inasmuch as I am the apostle to the Gentiles,
I glory in my ministry in order to make my race jealous
and thus save some of them.
For if their rejection is the reconciliation of the world,
what will their acceptance be but life from the dead?
For the gifts and the call of God are irrevocable.
Just as you once disobeyed God
but have now received mercy because of their disobedience,
so they have now disobeyed in order that,
by virtue of the mercy shown to you,
they too may now receive mercy.
For God delivered all to disobedience,
that he might have mercy upon all.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

BROTHERS and sisters:
Watch carefully how you live,
not as foolish persons but as wise,
making the most of the opportunity,
because the days are evil.
Therefore, do not continue in ignorance,
but try to understand what is the will of the Lord.
And do not get drunk on wine, in which lies debauchery,
but be filled with the Spirit,
addressing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs,
singing and playing to the Lord in your hearts,
giving thanks always and for everything
in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ to God the Father.

SECOND READING (Heb 12:5-7, 11-13)
A reading from the Letter to the Hebrew:
BROTHERS and sisters,
You have forgotten the exhortation addressed to you as children:
"My son, do not disdain the discipline of the Lord
or lose heart when reproved by him;
for whom the Lord loves, he disciplines;
he scourges every son he acknowledges."
Endure your trials as "discipline";
God treats you as sons.
For what "son" is there whom his father does not discipline?
At the time,
all discipline seems a cause not for joy but for pain,
yet later it brings the peaceful fruit of righteousness
to those who are trained by it.
So strengthen your drooping hands and your weak knees.
Make straight paths for your feet,
that what is lame may not be disjointed but healed.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

BROTHERS and sisters:
You have not approached that which could be touched
and a blazing fire and gloomy darkness

and countless angels in festal gathering,
and the assembly of the firstborn enrolled in heaven,
and God the judge of all,
and the spirits of the just made perfect,
and Jesus, the mediator of a new covenant,
and the sprinkled blood that speaks more eloquently than
that of Abel.

If I tell the wicked, “O wicked one, you shall surely die,” on the testimony of two or three witnesses.’

he shall die for his guilt, Again, amen, I say to you,

Come, let us sing joyfully to the Lord;

“Harden not your hearts as at Meribah,
as in the day of Massah in the desert,
Where your fathers tempted me;
they tested me though they had seen my works.”—R.
SECOND READING (Rom 13:8-10)
A reading from the Letter of Saint Paul to the Romans:
BROTHERS and sisters:
Owe nothing to anyone, except to love one another;
for the one who loves another has fulfilled the law.
The commandments, “You shall not commit adultery;
you shall not kill; you shall not steal; you shall not covet,”
and whatever other commandment there may be,
are summed up in this saying, namely,
“You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
Love does no evil to the neighbor;
hence, love is the fulfillment of the law.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
ALLELUIA (2 Cor 5:19)
R. Alleluia, alleluia.
God was reconciling the world to himself in Christ
and entrusting to us the message of reconciliation.—R.

SECOND READING (Jas 2:1-5)
A reading from the Letter of Saint James:
MY brothers and sisters, show no partiality
as you adhere to the faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ.
For if a man with gold rings and fine clothes
comes into your assembly,
and a poor person in shabby clothes also comes in,
and you pay attention to the one wearing the fine clothes
and say, “Sit here, please,”
while you say to the poor one, “Stand there,” or “Sit at my feet,”
have you not made distinctions among yourselves
and become judges with evil designs?
Listen, my beloved brothers and sisters.
Did not God choose those who are poor in the world
to be rich in faith and heirs of the kingdom
that he promised to those who love him?
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

None of us lives for oneself, and no one dies for oneself.

so then, whether we live or die, we are the Lord's.
For this is why Christ died and came to life,
that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

BROTHERS and sisters:
Christ will be magnified in my body, whether by life or by death.
For to me life is Christ, and death is gain.
If I go on living in the flesh,
that means fruitful labor for me.
And I do not know which I shall choose.
I am caught between the two.
I long to depart this life and be with Christ,
for that is far better.
Yet that I remain in the flesh
is more necessary for your benefit.
Only, conduct yourselves in a way worthy of the gospel of Christ.

BELOVED:
Where jealousy and selfish ambition exist,
there is disorder and every foul practice.
But the wisdom from above is first of all pure,
then peaceable, gentle, compliant,
full of mercy and good fruits,
without inconstancy or insincerity.
And the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace
for those who cultivate peace.
Where do the wars
and where do the conflicts among you come from?
Is it not from your passions
that make war within your members?
You covet but do not possess.
You kill and envy but you cannot obtain;
you fight and wage war.
You do not possess because you do not ask.
You ask but do not receive,

BELOVED:
I remind you, to stir into flame
the gift of God that you have through the imposition of my hands.
For God did not give us a spirit of cowardice
but rather of power and love and self-control.
So do not be ashamed of your testimony to our Lord,
nor of me, a prisoner for his sake;
but bear your share of hardship for the gospel
with the strength that comes from God.
Take as your norm the sound words that you heard from me,
in the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
Guard this rich trust with the help of the Holy Spirit
that dwells within us.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

together with eternal glory.
This saying is trustworthy:
If we have died with him
we shall also live with him;
if we persevere
we shall also reign with him.
But if we deny him
he will deny us.

so that through me the proclamation might be completed
and all the Gentiles might hear it.
And I was rescued from the lion's mouth.
The Lord will rescue me from every evil threat
and will bring me safe to his heavenly kingdom.

and base before all the people, They widen their phylacteries and lengthen their tassels.
since you do not keep my ways, They love places of honor at banquets, seats of honor in synagogues,

O LORD, my heart is not proud,
nor are my eyes haughty;
I busy not myself with great things,
nor with things too sublime for me.—R.
Nay rather, I have stilled and quieted
my soul like a weaned child.
Like a weaned child on its mother's lap,
so is my soul within me.—R.
O Israel, hope in the LORD,
both now and forever.—R.
SECOND READING (1 Thes 2:7b-9, 13)
A reading from the first Letter of Saint Paul to the Thessalonians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
We were gentle among you, as a nursing mother cares for her children.
With such affection for you, we were determined to share with you
not only the gospel of God, but our very selves as well,
so dearly beloved had you become to us.
You recall, brothers and sisters, our toil and drudgery.
Working night and day in order not to burden any of you,
we proclaimed to you the gospel of God.
And for this reason we too give thanks to God unceasingly,
that, in receiving the word of God from hearing us,
you received not a human word but, as it truly is, the word of God,
which is now at work in you who believe.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

SECOND READING (Heb 7:23-28)
A reading from the Letter to the Hebrews:
BROTHERS and sisters:
The levitical priests were many
because they were prevented by death from remaining in office,
but Jesus, because he remains forever,
has a priesthood that does not pass away.
Therefore, he is always able to save those who approach God through him,
since he lives forever to make intercession for them.
It was fitting that we should have such a high priest:
holy, innocent, undefiled, separated from sinners,
higher than the heavens.
He has no need, as did the high priests,
to offer sacrifice day after day,
first for his own sins and then for those of the people;
he did that once for all when he offered himself.
For the law appoints men subject to weakness to be high priests,
but the word of the oath, which was taken after the law,

the delights at your right hand forever.—R.
SECOND READING (Heb 10:11-14, 18)
A reading from the Letter to the Hebrews:
BROTHERS and sisters:
Every priest stands daily at his ministry,
offering frequently those same sacrifices
that can never take away sins.
But this one offered one sacrifice for sins,
and took his seat forever at the right hand of God;
now he waits until his enemies are made his footstool.
For by one offering
he has made perfect forever those who are being consecrated.
Where there is forgiveness of these,
there is no longer offering for sin.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.

and in him all things hold together.
He is the head of the body, the church.
He is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead,
that in all things he himself might be preeminent.
For in him all the fullness was pleased to dwell,
and through him to reconcile all things for him,
making peace by the blood of his cross

Then the angel departed from her.
BROTHERS and sisters:
It is impossible that the blood of bulls and goats The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
take away sins.
For this reason, when Christ came into the world, he said:
“Sacrifice and offering you did not desire,
but a body you prepared for me;
in holocausts and sin offerings you took no delight.
Then I said, ‘As is written of me in the scroll,
behold, I come to do your will, O God.’”
First he says, “Sacrifices and offerings,
holocausts and sin offerings,
you neither desired nor delighted in.”
These are offered according to the law.
Then he says, “Behold, I come to do your will.”
He takes away the first to establish the second.

HOLY TRINITY—A (Sunday after Pentecost)

FIRST READING (Ex 34:4b-6, 8-9) + A reading from the holy Gospel according to John:
A reading from the Book of Exodus: GOD so loved the world that he gave his only Son,
so that everyone who believes in him might not perish
EARLY in the morning Moses went up Mount Sinai but might have eternal life.
as the LORD had commanded him, For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world,
taking along the two stone tablets. but that the world might be saved through him.
Whoever believes in him will not be condemned,
Having come down in a cloud, the LORD stood with Moses there but whoever does not believe has already been condemned,
and proclaimed his name, "LORD." because he has not believed in the name of the only Son of God.
Thus the LORD passed before him and cried out,
"The LORD, the LORD, a merciful and gracious God, The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
slow to anger and rich in kindness and fidelity."
Moses at once bowed down to the ground in worship.
Then he said, "If I find favor with you, O Lord,
do come along in our company.
This is indeed a stiff-necked people; yet pardon our wickedness and sins,
and receive us as your own."
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
RESPONSORIAL PSALM (Dn 3:52, 53, 54, 55, 56)
R. (52b) Glory and praise for ever!
Blessed are you, O Lord, the God of our fathers,
praiseworthy and exalted above all forever;
And blessed is your holy and glorious name,
praiseworthy and exalted above all for all ages.—R.
Blessed are you in the temple of your holy glory,
praiseworthy and glorious above all forever.—R.
Blessed are you on the throne of your kingdom,
praiseworthy and exalted above all forever.—R.
Blessed are you who look into the depths
from your throne upon the cherubim,
praiseworthy and exalted above all forever.—R.
SECOND READING (2 Cor 13:11-13)
A reading from second Letter of Saint Paul to the Corinthians:
BROTHERS and sisters, rejoice. Mend your ways,
encourage one another,
agree with one another, live in peace,
and the God of love and peace will be with you.
Greet one another with a holy kiss.
All the holy ones greet you.
The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ
and the love of God
and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit be with all of you.

RESPONSORIAL PSALM (Psalm 33:4-5, 6, 9, 18-19, 20, 22)
R. (12b) Blessed the people the Lord has chosen to be his own.
Upright is the word of the LORD,
and all his works are trustworthy.
He loves justice and right;
of the kindness of the Lord the earth is full.—R.
By the word of the Lord the heavens were made;
by the breath of his mouth all their host.
For he spoke, and it was made;
he commanded, and it stood forth.—R.
See, the eyes of the Lord are upon those who fear him,
upon those who hope for his kindness,
To deliver them from death
and preserve them in spite of famine.—R.
Our soul waits for the Lord,
who is our help and our shield.
May your kindness, O Lord, be upon us
who have put our hope in you.—R.

For those who are led by the Spirit of God are sons of God.
For you did not receive a spirit of slavery to fall back into fear,
but you received a Spirit of adoption,
through whom we cry, "Abba, Father!"

THUS says the wisdom of God:
"The LORD possessed me, the beginning of his ways, R. Alleluia, alleluia.
the forerunner of his prodigies of long ago;
from of old I was poured forth, Glory to the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit;
at the first, before the earth. to God who is, who was, and who is to come.—R.
When there were no depths I was brought forth,
when there were no fountains or springs of water; GOSPEL (Jn 16:12-15)
before the mountains were settled into place,
before the hills, I was brought forth; + A reading from the holy Gospel according to John:
while as yet the earth and fields were not made,
nor the first clods of the world. JESUS said to his disciples:
"I have much more to tell you, but you cannot bear it now.
"When the Lord established the heavens I was there, But when he comes, the Spirit of truth,
when he marked out the vault over the face of the deep; he will guide you to all truth.
when he made firm the skies above, He will not speak on his own,
when he fixed fast the foundations of the earth; but he will speak what he hears,
when he set for the sea its limit, and will declare to you the things that are coming.
so that the waters should not transgress his command; He will glorify me,
then was I beside him as his craftsman, because he will take from what is mine and declare it to you.
and I was his delight day by day, Everything that the Father has is mine;
playing before him all the while, for this reason I told you that he will take from what is mine
playing on the surface of his earth; and declare it to you."
and I found delight in the human race."
The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
RESPONSORIAL PSALM (Psalm 8:4-5, 6-7, 8-9)
R. (2a) O Lord, our God, how wonderful your name in all the earth!
When I behold your heavens, the work of your fingers,
the moon and the stars which you set in place—
What is man that you should be mindful of him,
or the son of man that you should care for him?—R.
You have made him little less than the angels,
and crowned him with glory and honor.
You have given him rule over the works of your hands,
putting all things under his feet:—R.
All sheep and oxen,
yes, and the beasts of the field,
The birds of the air, the fishes of the sea,
and whatever swims the paths of the seas.—R.
SECOND READING (Rom 5:1-5)
A reading from the Letter of Saint Paul to the Romans:
BROTHERS and sisters:
Therefore, since we have been justified by faith,
we have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ,
through whom we have gained access by faith
to this grace in which we stand,
we boast in hope of the glory of God.
Not only that, but we even boast of our afflictions,
knowing that affliction produces endurance,
and endurance, proven character,

From his hands of old partaken,
IN those days, Melchizedek, king of Salem, brought out bread and wine, As we know, by faith unshaken,
and being a priest of God Most High, Where the Twelve at supper met.
he blessed Abram with these words:

for we are in a deserted place here."

unless we ourselves go and buy food for all these people."

Then he said to his disciples,
"Have them sit down in groups of about fifty."

A reading from the first Letter of Saint John:
BELOVED, let us love one another,
because love is of God;
everyone who loves is begotten by God and knows God.
Whoever is without love does not know God, for God is love.
In this way the love of God was revealed to us:
God sent his only Son into the world
so that we might have life through him.
In this is love:
not that we have loved God, but that he loved us
and sent his Son as expiation for our sins.

through all the earth their voice resounds, He said this signifying by what kind of death he would glorify God.

SECOND READING (Gal 1:11-20) The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
A reading from the Letter of Saint Paul to the Galatians:
I WANT you to know, brothers and sisters,
that the Gospel preached by me is not of human origin.
For I did not receive it from a human being, nor was I taught it,
but it came through a revelation of Jesus Christ.
For you heard of my former way of life in Judaism,
how I persecuted the Church of God beyond measure
and tried to destroy it, and progressed in Judaism
beyond many of my contemporaries among my race,
since I was even more a zealot for my ancestral traditions.
But when God, who from my mother’s womb had set me apart
and called me through his grace,
was pleased to reveal his Son to me,
so that I might proclaim him to the Gentiles,

IN those days, King Herod laid hands upon some members of the Church I have competed well; I have finished the race;
to harm them. I have kept the faith.

through our Lord Jesus Christ.

While he slew them they sought him
and inquired after God again,
Remembering that God was their rock
and the Most High God, their redeemer.—R.

and lied to him with their tongues,
Though their hearts were not steadfast toward him,
nor were they faithful to his covenant.—R.
But he, being merciful, forgave their sin
and destroyed them not;
Often he turned back his anger
and let none of his wrath be roused.—R.
SECOND READING (Phil 2:6-11)
A reading from the Letter of Saint Paul to the Philippians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
Christ Jesus, though he was in the form of God,
did not regard equality with God something to be grasped.
Rather, he emptied himself,
taking the form of a slave,
coming in human likeness;
and found human in appearance,
he humbled himself,
becoming obedient to death,
even death on a cross.

who were given power to damage the land and the sea, what we shall be has not yet been revealed.

from every tribe of the children of Israel.

that he might be Lord of both the dead and the living. The sting of death is sin,
