THAT very day, the first day of the week,
+ A reading from the holy Gospel according to Luke: two of Jesus' disciples were going
to a village seven miles from Jerusalem called Emmaus,
JESUS journeyed to a city called Nain, and they were conversing about all the things that had occurred.
and his disciples and a large crowd accompanied him. And it happened that while they were conversing and debating,
As he drew near to the gate of the city, Jesus himself drew near and walked with them,
a man who had died was being carried out, but their eyes were prevented from recognizing him.
the only son of his mother, and she was a widow. As they approached the village to which they were going,
A large crowd from the city was with her. he gave the impression that he was going on farther.
When the Lord saw her, But they urged him, "Stay with us,
he was moved with pity for her and said to her, for it is nearly evening and the day is almost over."
"Do not weep." So he went in to stay with them.
He stepped forward and touched the coffin; And it happened that, while he was with them at table,
at this the bearers halted, he took bread, said the blessing,
and he said, "Young man, I tell you, arise!" broke it, and gave it to them.
The dead man sat up and began to speak, With that their eyes were opened and they recognized him,
and Jesus gave him to his mother. but he vanished from their sight.
Fear seized them all, and they glorified God, exclaiming, Then they said to each other,
"A great prophet has arisen in our midst," "Were not our hearts burning within us
and "God has visited his people." while he spoke to us on the way and opened the Scriptures
This report about him spread through the whole of Judea to us?"
and in all the surrounding region. So they set out at once and returned to Jerusalem
where they found gathered together

And Jesus raised his eyes and said,
"Father, I thank you for hearing me.
I know that you always hear me;
but because of the crowd here I have said this,
that they may believe that you sent me."
And when he had said this,
he cried out in a loud voice,
"Lazarus, come out!"
The dead man came out,
tied hand and foot with burial bands,
and his face was wrapped in a cloth.
So Jesus said to them,
"Untie him and let him go."
Now many of the Jews who had come to Mary
and seen what he had done began to believe in him.
The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.
12. (Jn 14:1-6)
+ A reading from the holy Gospel according to John:
JESUS said to his disciples:
"Do not let your hearts be troubled.
You have faith in God; have faith also in me.
In my Father's house there are many dwelling places.
If there were not,
would I have told you that I am going to prepare
a place for you?
And if I go and prepare a place for you,
I will come back again and take you to myself,
so that where I am you also may be.
Where I am going you know the way."
Thomas said to him,
"Master, we do not know where you are going;
how can we know the way?"
Jesus said to him, "I am the way and the truth and the life.
No one comes to the Father except through me."
The Gospel of the Lord.—R. Praise to you, Lord Jesus Christ.

CATHOLIC SUNDAY READINGS Do you not know that you are the temple of God,
DEDICATION OF ST. JOHN LATERAN BASILICA and that the Spirit of God dwells in you?
—ABC (November 9) If anyone destroys God’s temple,
God will destroy that person;
for the temple of God, which you are, is holy.
FIRST READING (Ez 47:1-2, 8-9, 12)
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
A reading from the Book of the Prophet Ezekiel:
ALLELUIA (2 Chronicles 7:16)
THE angel brought me
back to the entrance of the temple, R. Alleluia, alleluia.
and I saw water flowing out
from beneath the threshold of the temple toward the east, I have chosen and consecrated this house, says the Lord,
for the façade of the temple was toward the east; that my name may be there forever.—R.
the water flowed down from the southern side of the temple,
south of the altar. GOSPEL (Jn 2:13-22)
He led me outside by the north gate,
and around to the outer gate facing the east, + A reading from the holy Gospel according to John:
where I saw water trickling from the southern side.
He said to me, SINCE the Passover of the Jews was near,
“This water flows into the eastern district down upon the Arabah, Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
and empties into the sea, the salt waters, which it makes fresh. He found in the temple area those who sold oxen, sheep, and doves,
Wherever the river flows, as well as the money-changers seated there.
every sort of living creature that can multiply shall live, He made a whip out of cords
and there shall be abundant fish, and drove them all out of the temple area, with the sheep and oxen,
for wherever this water comes the sea shall be made fresh. and spilled the coins of the money-changers
Along both banks of the river, fruit trees of every kind shall grow; and overturned their tables,
their leaves shall not fade, nor their fruit fail. and to those who sold doves he said,
Every month they shall bear fresh fruit, “Take these out of here,
for they shall be watered by the flow from the sanctuary. and stop making my Father’s house a marketplace.”
Their fruit shall serve for food, and their leaves for medicine.” His disciples recalled the words of Scripture,
Zeal for your house will consume me.
The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God. At this the Jews answered and said to him,
“What sign can you show us for doing this?”

The LORD of hosts is with us;
our stronghold is the God of Jacob.
Come! behold the deeds of the LORD,
the astounding things he has wrought on earth.—R.
SECOND READING (1 Cor 3:9c-11, 16-17)
A reading from the first Letter of Saint Paul to the Corinthians:
BROTHERS and sisters:
You are God’s building.
According to the grace of God given to me,
like a wise master builder I laid a foundation,
and another is building upon it.
But each one must be careful how he builds upon it,
for no one can lay a foundation other than the one that is there,
namely, Jesus Christ.

CATHOLIC SUNDAY READINGS as he chose us in him, before the foundation of the world,
IMMACULATE CONCEPTION—ABC (December 8) to be holy and without blemish before him.
In love he destined us for adoption to himself through Jesus Christ,
in accord with the favor of his will,
FIRST READING (Gn 3:9-15, 20) for the praise of the glory of his grace
that he granted us in the beloved.
A reading from the Book of Genesis:
In him we were also chosen,
AFTER the man, Adam, had eaten of the tree, destined in accord with the purpose of the One
the LORD God called to the man and asked him, “Where are you?” who accomplishes all things according to the intention of his will,
He answered, “I heard you in the garden; so that we might exist for the praise of his glory,
but I was afraid, because I was naked, we who first hoped in Christ.
so I hid myself.”
Then he asked, “Who told you that you were naked? The word of the Lord.—R. Thanks be to God.
You have eaten, then,
from the tree of which I had forbidden you to eat!” ALLELUIA (See Luke 1:28)

she gave me fruit from the tree, and so I ate it.” R. Alleluia, alleluia.

“Why did you do such a thing?” Hail, Mary, full of grace, the Lord is with you;
